---
title: Descriptive Statistics
description: Summarize data with center and spread.
---

::: {.learning-objectives}
### Learning Objectives

- Compute mean, median, variance, and standard deviation.
- Interpret center and spread.
- Use descriptive summaries carefully.
:::

```{python}
from edumath.statistics.concepts import describe

describe([1, 2, 3, 4, 10])
```

::: {.practice-box}
1. Which is more sensitive to outliers: mean or median?
2. What does standard deviation measure?
3. Why should summaries be paired with plots?
:::

## Solutions

::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"}
## Solution 1

Answer: mean.

The mean uses every value directly, so an extreme value can pull it strongly.
:::

::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"}
## Solution 2

Answer: typical spread around the mean.

A larger standard deviation means values tend to be farther from the mean.
:::

::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"}
## Solution 3

Answer: plots reveal shape, clusters, skew, and outliers that a few summary
numbers can hide.
:::
